raw_doc: CEO Jane Wu: Good morning, everyone. I'm pleased to report another strong quarter. We expect full-year revenue to be in the range of $2.8 to $3.0 billion, representing approximately 20% growth over fiscal 2023. We are on track to achieve non-GAAP operating margins of 18-20% by Q4. Our strategic partnership with NVIDIA on AI inference chips positions us well for the next several years. Analyst: Can you give more color on the margin expansion path? CFO: We remain confident in the 18-20% target. The primary drivers will be manufacturing yield improvements and SG&A leverage as we scale.

forward_statements: We expect full-year revenue to be in the range of $2.8 to $3.0 billion, representing approximately 20% growth over fiscal 2023
We are on track to achieve non-GAAP operating margins of 18-20% by Q4
Our strategic partnership with NVIDIA on AI inference chips positions us well for the next several years
We remain confident in the 18-20% target

prepared_remarks_tone.rubric_note: Clearly optimistic prepared remarks with explicit revenue and margin guidance and positive strategic narrative.

q_and_a_evasiveness.rubric_note: CFO directly reaffirms margin target and cites specific drivers; low evasiveness score warranted.

rationale: Tests four forward-statements including a reaffirmation in Q&A; both subjective fields have high confidence.

raw_doc: CFO Michael Torres: Before I walk through the numbers, I want to acknowledge the macro uncertainty. We anticipate revenue of $410 to $440 million next quarter. Analyst: Can you comment on the China business? CEO Sarah Chen: We're monitoring the situation carefully. There are a number of factors at play, including regulatory dynamics and customer inventory normalization. At this point we're not in a position to size the impact, but we continue to engage constructively with our local partners. Analyst: Any update on the timeline for the new product launch? CEO: We remain on track for a mid-2025 commercial launch, which we believe will open a significant addressable market.

forward_statements: We anticipate revenue of $410 to $440 million next quarter
We remain on track for a mid-2025 commercial launch, which we believe will open a significant addressable market

prepared_remarks_tone.rubric_note: Mixed-tone prepared remarks: CFO signals macro uncertainty while providing a revenue range, suggesting cautious-neutral framing.

q_and_a_evasiveness.rubric_note: CEO clearly dodges the China impact question — uses hedging language and refuses to size the impact, which is textbook evasive behavior.

rationale: Tests Q&A evasiveness contrast with direct CFO forward guidance; illustrates that subjective scores for the two fields can diverge.

raw_doc: CEO David Park: Our cloud segment grew 47% year-over-year to $890 million in Q3. We are raising our full-year cloud revenue guidance to $3.4 to $3.5 billion from the prior $3.2 to $3.4 billion range. We also expect to reach free-cash-flow positivity on an annualized basis by the end of Q1 2026. We are expanding our partnership with Salesforce to offer integrated CRM and analytics solutions, which we project will contribute $150 million in incremental revenue over the next 18 months. Analyst: What is your hiring plan? CFO: We plan to add approximately 800 net new employees in technical and sales roles over the next two quarters.

forward_statements: We are raising our full-year cloud revenue guidance to $3.4 to $3.5 billion from the prior $3.2 to $3.4 billion range
We also expect to reach free-cash-flow positivity on an annualized basis by the end of Q1 2026
We are expanding our partnership with Salesforce to offer integrated CRM and analytics solutions, which we project will contribute $150 million in incremental revenue over the next 18 months
We plan to add approximately 800 net new employees in technical and sales roles over the next two quarters

prepared_remarks_tone.rubric_note: Highly optimistic — management raises guidance, announces a significant partnership expansion, and cites strong cloud growth metrics.

q_and_a_evasiveness.rubric_note: CFO provides a specific hiring figure with a clear timeframe; very low evasiveness.

rationale: Tests guidance raise and partnership projection as forward statements; tests concrete Q&A answer for low-evasiveness scoring.

raw_doc: CEO Amanda Foster: We are not providing specific revenue or earnings guidance at this time given the ongoing review of strategic alternatives. We are committed to maximizing shareholder value and will communicate any updates when appropriate. Our balance sheet remains strong with $1.2 billion in cash. Analyst: Can you comment on the strategic review timeline? CEO: We have engaged financial advisors and are running a thorough process. I'm not able to provide a timeline or comment on the nature of the alternatives being evaluated. Analyst: Are you considering a sale of the company? CEO: As I said, we're not going to comment on the specifics of the process.

prepared_remarks_tone.rubric_note: Deliberately non-committal prepared remarks — management explicitly withholds guidance due to strategic review, a classically defensive posture.

q_and_a_evasiveness.rubric_note: CEO refuses to answer two consecutive direct questions about timeline and transaction type; highly evasive.

rationale: Tests the empty forward_statements case (no scorable forward guidance during a strategic review); both subjective fields are high confidence.

raw_doc: CFO Raj Patel: Gross margin in Q2 came in at 54.2%, up 180 basis points sequentially due to favorable product mix. We expect gross margins of 53-55% in Q3. Operating expenses were $210 million, and we are guiding to $215-220 million in Q3 as we increase R&D investment. Analyst: Any update on the new data-center product line? VP of Products Lisa Huang: We expect to begin customer shipments in late Q4 2024, with volume ramp into Q1 2025. Initial feedback from early-access customers has been very positive and we see a path to $200 million in annual revenue from this product line within two years.

forward_statements: We expect gross margins of 53-55% in Q3
we are guiding to $215-220 million in Q3 as we increase R&D investment
We expect to begin customer shipments in late Q4 2024, with volume ramp into Q1 2025
we see a path to $200 million in annual revenue from this product line within two years

prepared_remarks_tone.rubric_note: Factual and measured prepared remarks with incremental positive signals; tone is neutral-to-positive, focused on financial metrics.

q_and_a_evasiveness.rubric_note: Product VP gives specific timeline, ramp schedule, and revenue projection; directly responsive with no hedging.

rationale: Tests product-launch timeline and revenue projection as forward statements alongside margin and OpEx guidance.

raw_doc: CEO Thomas Briggs: The semiconductor shortage that constrained our production in 2023 has largely resolved, and we are well-positioned for a strong second half. We expect to exit the year with a backlog of $1.4 billion, and anticipate converting approximately 70% of that backlog into revenue over the next four quarters. Analyst: What is driving the inventory normalization? VP Supply Chain: Our Tier-1 suppliers have largely worked through their excess inventory positions. We expect lead times to normalize to pre-pandemic levels by mid-2025. Analyst: Could macro weakness derail the recovery? CEO: Obviously macro is a risk we monitor, but our current order trends give us confidence in the outlook.

forward_statements: We expect to exit the year with a backlog of $1.4 billion, and anticipate converting approximately 70% of that backlog into revenue over the next four quarters
We expect lead times to normalize to pre-pandemic levels by mid-2025

prepared_remarks_tone.rubric_note: Constructive and optimistic prepared remarks emphasizing recovery from supply-chain headwinds and a strong order backlog.

q_and_a_evasiveness.rubric_note: CEO acknowledges macro risk but pivots to order trends — partially evasive rather than directly answering the downside scenario.

rationale: Tests supply-chain sector language and two forward statements; Q&A evasiveness is medium confidence due to a partial pivot response.

raw_doc: CEO Patricia Okafor: The regulatory environment for our digital-asset custody platform has evolved significantly. We have received conditional approval from the OCC to operate as a national trust company, which we expect to finalize in early 2025. This approval will enable us to expand our institutional client base across all 50 states. We project onboarding 40-60 new institutional clients over the next 12 months and generating $80-100 million in custody fee revenue by year-end 2025. Analyst: What happens if the OCC approval is delayed further? CEO: We are prepared for that scenario and have a state-by-state licensing strategy as a contingency.

forward_statements: which we expect to finalize in early 2025
We project onboarding 40-60 new institutional clients over the next 12 months and generating $80-100 million in custody fee revenue by year-end 2025

prepared_remarks_tone.rubric_note: Optimistic but conditional tone — strong expansion projections are contingent on regulatory approval, creating measured forward-looking language.

q_and_a_evasiveness.rubric_note: CEO acknowledges the downside scenario and describes a contingency plan, which is responsive but does not address probability or timeline of a delay.

rationale: Fintech/digital-asset sector sample with regulatory-conditional forward statements; medium Q&A evasiveness due to partial contingency answer.
